name: Month-End Close
tagline: A deterministic month-end close that cannot silently go wrong — ten controls, proven
plain_summary: The engine runs a full month-end close on a fictional three-entity group: it computes nine classes of recurring journal entry (prepaid amortization, depreciation, deferred rent and CAM, fixed-fee and management-fee accruals, note interest, G&A and insurance allocations, and exact-route postage), ties every backing schedule back to the ledger, and writes a JE register, an updated trial balance, and a close report. Before the close counts as clean, ten independent Close Sentinel controls re-check it for the ways a month-end silently goes wrong — a missed accrual, a one-sided intercompany entry, an asset depreciating past its life — and a CRITICAL finding blocks the close and fails the run's exit code. All money is integer cents so tie-outs are exact, and every run is seeded and reproducible.
marketing_thesis: A controller cannot take "the close is clean" on faith. Every figure on this page re-derives from the public repository by running the commands shown — detection is proven, not asserted.
layers_intro: Each layer is an independent stage of the close, stacked from the pipeline gate down to the seeded data. Select a layer for its plain-terms and engineering detail.

layers.label: CLI & exit-code gate
layers.plain: Runs the close and turns the result into a green/red pipeline signal — it only reports success when the close is genuinely clean.
layers.engineering: argparse CLI (main() -> exit code). Flags: --period, --out, --seed, --sentinel/--no-sentinel, --demo-guardrails, --version. Exit is non-zero on any refused entry, a failed schedule tie, an unbalanced trial balance, or any CRITICAL Sentinel finding, so it can gate CI.

layers.label: Close Sentinel — ten independent controls
layers.plain: An independent referee that re-checks the finished close for the ten classic ways a month-end silently goes wrong, and blocks it if any fire.
layers.engineering: ALL_CONTROLS = C1..C10 (re-balance, intercompany mirror, completeness calendar, asset-life guard, driver provenance, cross-foot, step-change, rounding policy, shadow recompute, period lock). Findings graded CRITICAL/WARN/INFO; C9 re-derives every amount from an implementation that imports nothing from the engine. The close is clean only when no CRITICAL finding exists.

layers.label: Recurring-entry engine & tie-out
layers.plain: Computes the nine recurring journal entries each month and ties every backing schedule back to the ledger before anything counts.
layers.engineering: CloseEngine.run() loads the opening TB, refuses invalid split maps and postage batches up front, posts nine categories in fixed order, then _tie_out() reconciles each schedule to its GL account (prepaid to acct 1400, fixed-fee to 2350, insurance to 1450, postage to 1460).

layers.label: Accounting model & balance control
layers.plain: The chart of accounts, journal entries, and ledger — enforcing the hard rule that debits equal credits, in total and within each entity leg.
layers.engineering: Ledger.post() raises OutOfTieError unless the entry balances in aggregate and (for intercompany) per entity. JournalEntry.balances_per_entity(); Ledger.trial_balance() and total_debits_credits() report signed integer cents.

layers.label: Integer-cent money primitives
layers.plain: All money is whole cents, so splits and allocations always add back to the exact total with no penny lost or invented.
layers.engineering: to_cents (round-half-up), split_evenly (remainder lands on the final period), allocate_by_ratio (largest-remainder / Hamilton; raises unless weights sum to exactly 10000 bps). No float accumulation.

layers.label: Seeded synthetic data generator
layers.plain: Builds a fictional three-entity group and its sub-ledgers from a fixed seed, so every run is identical and re-derivable.
layers.engineering: generate_dataset(period, seed) emits entities DH/MF/BW, an opening trial balance, and prepaid/asset/lease/note/insurance/postage sub-ledgers using the stdlib random module only — no pandas, numpy, or faker.

blocks.label: Seeded dataset
blocks.engineering: generate_dataset(period, seed=2026): entities, opening TB, prepaids, assets, leases, notes, insurance, postage.

blocks.label: Integer-cent math
blocks.engineering: split_evenly, allocate_by_ratio (largest-remainder; weights sum to 10000 bps).

blocks.label: Recurring-entry engine
blocks.engineering: CloseEngine.run(): refuse invalid splits/postage, post 9 categories, balance gate per entity.

blocks.label: Ledger + balance control
blocks.plain: Debits must equal credits, per entity too
blocks.engineering: Ledger.post() raises OutOfTieError on aggregate or per-entity imbalance.

blocks.label: Read-only preflight family
blocks.engineering: recurring_register, tb_package, group_operations, project_job_cost, personal_property_tax, payroll_recovery, headcount, je_package, budget_variance, cash_draw, trial_balance, audit_pbc, consolidation — never post.

blocks.label: Schedule to GL tie-out
blocks.plain: Each schedule must reconcile to its account
blocks.engineering: _tie_out() -> TieResult per schedule; prepaid ties to acct 1400, fixed-fee to 2350, insurance to 1450, postage to 1460.

blocks.label: Shadow recompute (C9)
blocks.plain: A second, independent computation of every amount
blocks.engineering: sentinel/shadow.py re-derives (entity, category, account) -> (dr, cr) importing nothing from the engine; the posted register must match to the cent.

blocks.label: Close Sentinel C1-C10
blocks.plain: Ten controls block a silent close error
blocks.engineering: run_sentinel(dataset, result) -> SentinelReport; any CRITICAL => not clean.

blocks.label: Register / TB / report
blocks.plain: Writes JE register, trial balance, close report
blocks.engineering: write_outputs(): je_register.md/.json, trial_balance.md/.json, schedules.json, close_report.md, sentinel.json.

blocks.label: Autonomous close loop
blocks.plain: Auto-remediates in-period drift, quarantines what it cannot certify
blocks.engineering: autonomous_close_loop(): observe -> detect -> remediate -> re-verify -> gate; resync the earliest drifted category from the seeded source of record; verdict AUTO-POSTED / AUTO-POSTED (PARTIAL) / HALTED. Deterministic and offline — no model calls.

blocks.label: Verdict + exit-code gate
blocks.engineering: cli.main() and loop.verdict_exit_code(); a CRITICAL finding, a refused entry, or a failed tie => exit 1.

edges.label: C9: must agree to the cent

spec_strip.label: Recurring entries automated
spec_strip.plain: Nine kinds of month-end journal entry, each with a backing schedule
spec_strip.source: monthly-close-automation/README.md 'What the engine computes' table; close_engine/engine.py run() posts 9 categories

spec_strip.label: Deterministic controls
spec_strip.plain: Ten independent checks; a single CRITICAL blocks the close

spec_strip.label: Fault-injection coverage
spec_strip.plain: Twelve classic close errors, each caught by its mapped control
spec_strip.source: close_engine/faults.py FAULTS dict; verified via `python -m close_engine --demo-guardrails` -> 'ALL GUARDRAILS HELD' (exit 0)

spec_strip.label: Tests
spec_strip.plain: Tests pytest collects for this engine
spec_strip.source: python -m pytest --collect-only -q (monthly-close-automation/); verified 2026-07-20 and pinned in site-datasheets/counts.json

spec_strip.label: Money precision
spec_strip.plain: All amounts are whole cents; allocations use largest-remainder

spec_strip.label: Demo entity group
spec_strip.plain: A fictional three-entity group generated from a fixed seed

problem_statement: Month-end close at a large entity group is a recurring operational burden: many interdependent recurring journal entries, each with its own logic, spread across multiple operating entities and separate accounting databases. Run from memory or a static spreadsheet, errors accumulate and the close extends for days. The failures that hurt most are the silent ones — a recurring accrual that quietly stops posting, a fully depreciated asset that keeps depreciating, an allocation driven by a cumulative balance instead of the month's activity. Each looks exactly like a clean close until someone finds it quarters later.

scenarios.title: A clean March close
scenarios.narrative: Running the close for 2026-03 posts all nine recurring entries balanced, ties every schedule to the GL (prepaid 8,100.00, fixed-fee 62,250.00, insurance 10,950.00, and postage account 1460 clearing to 0.00 in each entity), lands the trial balance at Dr 3,603,311.50 = Cr 3,603,311.50, and Sentinel reports zero findings — close status CLEAN, exit 0.
scenarios.source: monthly-close-automation/README.md 'Real example output' console summary + output/close_report.md; re-run: python -m close_engine --period 2026-03 --out ./output

scenarios.title: Prove the controls with injected faults
scenarios.narrative: The guardrail demo first runs a clean baseline (zero findings), then injects twelve classic close errors one at a time — an unbalanced opening, an ended asset still depreciating, a one-sided intercompany entry, a missing accrual, a rounding drift, a single-cent shadow tamper, a rewritten locked period, and more — and asserts each is caught by exactly the control designed for it. Verdict: ALL GUARDRAILS HELD (12 faults, baseline clean), exit 0.

scenarios.title: Autonomous remediation of a drifted close
scenarios.narrative: Given a close contaminated with a drift profile (19 initial findings), the Autonomous Close Loop resyncs four drifted categories from the seeded sub-ledger of record over four turns (criticals fall 18 -> 1), quarantines the tampered locked prior period it has no authority to overwrite, and posts the current period on its own authority — verdict AUTO-POSTED (PARTIAL), exit 0, with a byte-identical settled register.
scenarios.source: monthly-close-automation/README.md 'Autonomous Close Loop' + output/autonomous_close_loop.md; re-run: python -m close_engine.loop --demo --out output

instruction_set.output: Prints the fictional entity group and sub-ledgers

instruction_set.exit_code: 0 clean; 1 if refused / failed-tie / unbalanced / CRITICAL

instruction_set.operation: Same close via the convenience wrapper
Inject 12 faults; prove each is caught by its control

instruction_set.exit_code: 0 only if baseline clean AND all 12 caught

instruction_set.operation: Drift a close + a locked prior; run the autonomous loop
Read-only preflight of a recurring-JE register
instruction_set.artifacts: none; validation only (0 posting actions, 0 import payloads)

benchmarks.label: Guardrail fault coverage
benchmarks.plain: Every injected close error caught by its mapped control

benchmarks.plain: The loop clears in-period drift down to a single quarantined item and still posts

benchmarks.unit: meter rows routed; acct 1460 clears to 0.00 in every entity
benchmarks.plain: Four of five postage rows route to an approved job; the clearing account nets to zero
benchmarks.source: output/close_report.md tie-out; README console summary 'Postage routes : 4/5 meter rows'

control_characteristics.plain: The core is a posting engine: it computes and posts journal entries against seeded synthetic data, then gates the result behind ten deterministic controls and an exit code. It mutates no real source of record, and a mechanically clean result on the read-only preflights is only 'READY FOR HUMAN REVIEW' — controller sign-off stays a human gate.

authority.note: Action-authority precedence in the autonomous loop, not an evidence hierarchy. The loop has no authority to invent a broken opening carryforward, so it refuses to post and escalates to a human. HALT_CONTROLS = {C1} in loop.py; exit 1.

A signed-off, locked prior period was altered; the loop will not overwrite it — it holds and logs, and still posts the current period. QUARANTINE_CONTROLS = {C10} in loop.py; verdict AUTO-POSTED (PARTIAL).
The loop is authorized to resync a drifted recurring-entry category to the seeded sub-ledger of record. AUTO_REMEDIABLE = {C2,C3,C4,C5,C6,C7,C8,C9} in loop.py.

verdict_map.action: Close proceeds; flagged for a reviewer
Corroborated note kept in the audit trail
Resync the category from the source of record; exit 0
Quarantine + log; post the current period; exit 0
Read-only; never posts (0 posting actions / 0 import payloads)

modes.name: Authoritative close (posting)
modes.detail: CloseEngine.run() computes and posts the nine recurring JEs, writes the register / TB / report, and Sentinel gates the exit code (cli.py).

modes.name: Read-only preflight (validation)
modes.detail: 13 validation-only modules validate an external JSON package and return PASS / NEEDS REVIEW; they never create a JE, import payload, or posting action (recurring_register.py and siblings).

modes.name: Guardrail demo (proof)
modes.detail: --demo-guardrails injects 12 seeded faults and asserts each is caught by its mapped control at a qualifying severity (faults.py).

modes.name: Autonomous close loop (remediation)
modes.detail: loop.py drives a drifted close back to a certifiable state (observe -> remediate -> re-verify -> gate) with a deterministic, logged gate policy. It is always offline and deterministic, with no model calls.

guarantees: All money is integer cents; tie-outs are exact (money.py).
Every JE balances debits == credits AND each entity leg self-balances, or it is refused (model.Ledger.post raises OutOfTieError).
Allocation weights must sum to exactly 10000 bps (100.00%) or the entry is refused, never coerced (money.allocate_by_ratio; engine refuses invalid splits).
Largest-remainder (Hamilton) allocation and split_evenly: parts sum exactly to the total, with no penny lost or created (money.py).
C9 shadow recompute: a second implementation importing nothing from the engine must agree to the cent before the close stands (sentinel/shadow.py).
Byte-identical settled register: the autonomous loop's output matches a clean engine run; it never invents a number (loop.py).
Period lock (C10): each closed register is sealed with a hash, so a later mutation of a locked period is detected on recompute.
Fully seeded and reproducible for a given seed; refused entries are reported, not silently posted.
Non-zero exit code on any refused entry, failed schedule tie, unbalanced TB, or CRITICAL finding — so it can gate a pipeline (cli.py).

gate_policy.note: The posting engine runs against seeded synthetic data and never touches a real ERP or source of record. Every read-only preflight is explicit that a mechanically clean result is only 'READY FOR HUMAN REVIEW' — source refresh and tie, mapping, and controller sign-off remain manual human gates. The autonomous loop replaces the person only for deterministic, logged in-period remediation, and still HALTS on an opening imbalance (C1) and quarantines a locked-period tamper (C10) rather than acting unilaterally.

limits.statement: Single functional currency only — there is no FX translation layer, and no control asserts anything about rate selection or translation differences.
The 13 validation-only preflights never create a journal entry, import payload, posting action, or source-system mutation — a clean result is only 'READY FOR HUMAN REVIEW'.
limits.source: monthly-close-automation/README.md validation-only sections; close_engine/recurring_register.py and siblings

limits.statement: The autonomous loop refuses to post on a broken opening carryforward (HALT / C1) and never auto-overwrites a signed-off locked prior period (QUARANTINE / C10); it invents no numbers and its settled register is byte-identical to a clean run.
limits.source: close_engine/loop.py (HALT_CONTROLS, QUARANTINE_CONTROLS); README 'Autonomous does not mean ungated'

limits.statement: C9 proves dataset-to-register translation, not dataset truth: if the raw sub-ledger is wrong, both independent implementations agree on the same wrong number, and offsetting shifts within one (entity, category, account) GL cell are invisible to GL-level controls.

media.poster_alt: Animated overview tile for the Month-End Close engine — the close pipeline and its ten Close Sentinel controls rendered as brand motion on fictional seeded data.
media.caption: Animated overview of the close run on fictional seeded data; run the commands above to reproduce.

meta.description: Month-End Close (SFS-E01-CLS) datasheet — a deterministic, seeded month-end close engine that posts nine recurring journal-entry classes across a fictional three-entity group, ties every schedule to the ledger, and gates the result behind ten independent Close Sentinel controls; every figure re-derives from the public repository.
